rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION Calendar for Wednesday, Jun-10-2026. LINE 7. PETITIONER MIC-BRY8, LLC's Motion For Hearing On Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights. Off calendar. The court will sign the proposed order submitted by the transferee. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION Calendar for Wednesday, Jun-10-2026. LINE 2. DEFENDANTS ADVANCED BIONICS CORPORATION, ADVANCED BIONICS LLC, and MARY LEEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication. Defendants motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. JUNIPER CAFE, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION Calendar for Wednesday, Jun-10-2026. LINE 3. 1-PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Alexander Xue's demurrer to defendant Juniper Café LLC's answer to the First Amended Complaint is overruled. The answer states adequate facts to support affirmative defenses, the affirmative defenses are not uncertain or incomprehensible, and the answer adequately alleges for purposes of the pleadings the effect of any agreements by plaintiff. Any uncertainty can be cured in discovery. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. JUNIPER CAFE, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION Calendar for Wednesday, Jun-10-2026. LINE 3. 2-PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to strike portions of Juniper Café LLC's answer to the First Amended Complaint is denied. The first affirmative defense, failure to state a claim, is a recognized form of pleading. (Bracker v. American Nat. Food (1955) 133 Cal.App.2d 338, 339-340.) The eighth affirmative defense, mootness, adds facts concerning whether this action presents a live controversy. The ninth affirmative defense, that the action is frivolous and may be sanctionable under Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 128.7, is not a traditional affirmative defense, but it is not irrelevant, false, or improper within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 436, subdivision (a). The motion to strike is further denied as to the prayer for relief. No cross-complaint is required to seek attorneys' fees or sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
